summary: Closed, apparently unmerged PR proposing improvements to the split_suggester tooling plus Sheik function-name updates. The available slice has no changed files or diff, so details are limited to the PR body: console output previews would include map file names, generated function headers would omit duplicate floats, and generated include lines would use a relative path form instead of only "filename.h".
what_changed: Proposed split_suggester console output preview improvement: add map file names to the displayed output.
Proposed generated function-header cleanup: exclude duplicate floats from generated function headers.
Proposed include-generation change: emit include lines using a relative path form rather than only a bare header filename such as "filename.h".
PR body also says Sheik function names were renamed, but the slice contains no diff or file list to identify exact symbols.
smart_moves: Adding map file names to split_suggester output would make generated suggestions easier to trace back to their source map entries.
Filtering duplicate floats before generating function headers would reduce noisy or redundant generated declarations.
Using relative include paths would make generated headers less ambiguous when similarly named headers exist in different directories.
decomp_lessons: Tool-generated decomp scaffolding should preserve enough source context, such as map file names, for reviewers to verify suggestions quickly.
Header generation tools should deduplicate repeated inferred constants or float references before emitting declarations.
Generated #include lines should prefer project-relative paths when the destination header is not uniquely identifiable by basename alone.
Because this PR has no available diff and was closed without a recorded merge, treat these ideas as proposed practices rather than confirmed repository changes.
naming_conventions: The PR mentions renamed Sheik function names, but no exact old/new names are present in the available context.
The include-style change described in the PR body implies a preference for relative header paths over bare "filename.h" includes in generated output.
follow_up_queries: Find later merged PRs touching split_suggester to see whether map filename output, duplicate-float filtering, or relative include generation landed elsewhere.
Search for Sheik function renames around February 2023 to recover the exact naming changes absent from this PR slice.
Inspect the split_suggester implementation history for generated #include formatting and duplicate float handling.
